steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: The subject is “Such lists,” a plural noun. The intervening phrase “observed sharply yet simply by Shonagon, who served as a lady-in-waiting to Empress Teishi in the tenth century” is a participial phrase that modifies “lists” but does not constitute an additional subject or clause.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
The rule is subject-verb agreement: a plural subject requires a plural verb form. “Reveal” (choice D) is the plural form that answers this requirement.

Choices A and B are singular verb forms while choice C is an infinitive, violating subject-verb agreement.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
